review_title: Ocoya provides an all-in-one platform for creating, scheduling and tracking social media posts
description: So easy to use. I previously had to create social media graphics using Adobe Photoshop and Adobe Illustrator and then export the graphics from those programs and import them into a social media scheduling program like Hootsuite. this process took a long time, but with Ocoya, I was able to cut this production time in half.
author: Stephen B.
pros: I discovered Ocoya on Appsumo and originally purchased it on a whim without totally understanding how many things it can do. I spent an afternoon experimenting with it and I was amazed at how fast I would create a great-looking Facebook Fan Page post, by choosing the graphic, using the AI program to write the copy, and then scheduling and posting it. It took less than 30 minutes.
cons: I like everything with Ocoya, but there is one minor change that I hope with will add - The jubb.ly link shortener looks interesting, but I wish it was connected to the analytics section of Ocoya.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Ocoya: A Game Changer for Social Media Management
description: My overall experience with Ocoya has been highly positive. The platform's intuitive interface and AI-powered content creation tools have made managing social media much more efficient and enjoyable. The variety of templates and seamless scheduling across different platforms have significantly boosted my productivity and engagement. The analytics feature provides valuable insights that help refine my strategy. While the integration with tools like Canva and Shopify is excellent, the customer support response time could be quicker. Despite this minor issue, Ocoya has greatly enhanced my social media management process, and I highly recommend it.
author: Peter E.
pros: Ocoya has transformed my social media management with its intuitive design, AI-powered content creation, and streamlined analytics. It's an all-in-one solution that has improved my social media presence and efficiency
cons: Using Ocoya has revolutionized my social media management with its intuitive interface and AI-powered content creation, which saves me a lot of time. The versatile templates and seamless scheduling across platforms like Instagram and Facebook are fantastic, and the analytics provide valuable insights to refine my strategy. While the integration with tools like Canva and Shopify is excellent, the customer support response time could be quicker.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Gift for marketing department
author: Vahe T.
pros: Ocoya's AI-powered content creation capabilities. This feature can significantly streamline the content creation process, especially for businesses or individuals who may not have dedicated writing teams. By automating tasks like generating ideas, writing drafts, and optimizing posts for specific platforms.
cons: While Ocoya provides some basic analytics, some users would like to see more in-depth insights into their social media performance. Features like advanced audience demographics, competitor analysis, and sentiment analysis could be valuable additions.

description: In general, one commentary related to anagrams and the history of the Delia put Ocoya as a somewhat competent platform in terms of content creation as well as firm’s social media. The benefits awaiting center with its AI tools, templates and ability to schedule most of the processes. There are some minor concerns such as lack of customization or better integrations but it is worth for marketers and creators considering how productivity can be improved.
author: Mihthar M.
pros: Ocoya is unique in its capability to automate the process of content creation along with the effective management of social media. Its AI-powered technologies eliminate the need for composing, designing, or scheduling posts, making it an ideal all-in-one hub for marketers and creators who wish to accomplish more with less time.
cons: It may take new users some time to get used to it because it has a lot of features, and advanced users with specific requirements may find the lack of customization or integrations rather limiting at times.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

Do you want to upload in highest quality to instagram? Nup no way. Do you want to spend hundreds of dollars for a horrible software with zero customer support? Yes you are in luck. I wouldn’t recommend this to my worst enemy. Its horrible. STAY AWAY!

review_title: Spot On Combo of Social + Travis AI (Unlimited)
description: It's been real fun playing around the software. For me it's very important to see what the product has to offer, it's initial response, their roadmap and creator interest while serving their audience. I have been quite impressed with the product and will be using it down under on regular basis. Any product evolves over a period of time and their roadmap is promising too. So far I made the right choice and may stack few more codes.
author: Ashutosh A.
pros: 1. Very intuitive UI & UX along with smooth flow 2. Many options to choose from Image/Gif/Video/Sticker etc. 3. Schedule directly to social platform with confirmation on Ocoya calendar. 4. Unlimited Travis AI 5. 25 profile integration in basic solver plan is awesome (I purchased it when it was 20 not it's 25 Christmas gift...do consider that)
cons: 1. Report is missing big time (this kind of work has to be measurable). This need real and serious attention from the developers. 2. Post scheduling automation concrete test. 3. Travis AI - In some section (small brief to AI for generating content) we get only one suggestion from Travis it should give at least 2-3 options. 4. Increase workspace and include white label option
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Perfect For Scaling Businesses
description: It was quite reasonable to be honest. It seems like a project that is still in development with lot of loose ends and areas to improve on. But, it has nailed the fundamentals and created strong foundation of features and relevant tools for ease of almost all businesses.
author: Aditya A.
pros: My firm is growing rapidly, and we needed a service that can handle our current smaller needs along with the needs that grow everymonth. Till now, I had used solutions like FS-Poster to schedule my social media posts, but now Ocoya helps me achieve a lot more comfortably. I can connect multiple social media accounts. Most services have poor support for Instagram, especially Instagram Stories, but Ocoya is brilliant in handling and managing all social media platforms. My Ocoya is connected to Zapier, which helps me build custom 3rd party integrations and massively improve the scope of what I can achieve with this. My site is driven my WordPress, so Zapier helps link my WordPress to Ocoya for even better handling. Love it. I found the interface to be a bit complicated for a basic user, but the responsive customer support helped me navigate through it easily. Till now, our blog writers would write content and that would directly get shared on Social Media. Now, with Ocoya, I can do that automatically, but in addition their AI-driven tools create completely new content from scratch, helping us run targetted campaigns easily. It helped save us time, and also perform proper tracking and data gathering through shortlinks support. The multi-language support for AI generated content was valuable for us, since we are in a non-english speaking region.
cons: THere is not much to complaint about it. Sometimes the site can be a bit slow. I don't know why but in my experience it often has random hiccups. I would really appreciate for generating and handling images. Everyone knows Social Media is dominated by videos and images. Support for AI-driven videos and proper infographics/images would be really cool. (I am not a fan of their AI art)
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: An all-in-one powerhouse with promising traction
description: Excellent UX and UI experience, very easy to use, and a big time saver by combining assistive writing and graphic tools into one social media posting platform.
author: Michael C.
pros: We often use templates that are available in Canva to save time when designing posts. As a direct integration, we are able to load the completed Canva social post designs into Ocoya. This has saved us a lot of time. The Travis AI is a great tool for quickly creating captions, although the output is not always great, they can be easily edited and should be used as an assistive writing tool. I would like to see the AI outputs improved. Ocoya's UX and UI are great, and there is little onboarding or learning curve involved. A much more pleasant experience to use Ocoya over other apps such as Publer or Social Bee, which we have used in the past.
cons: Lack of workspaces and granular permissions for users. Inability to separate social accounts. AI outputs could be improved. As far as I know, all of these are being addressed, so I will begin moving more accounts to Ocoya once these issues are resolved.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good tool that needs some improvements
description: If we worked only with Twitter, Meta, Linkedin and posted unique posts - that would be fine. But we are often reusing content across different platforms and in this case it's a bit complicated
author: Yurii M.
pros: Lifetime deal with AppSumo. Overall the tool does what it has to do: it is integrated with the majority of social media platforms
cons: Missing integration with Google Business Profile and TikTok. There are disadvantages in terms of planning (for example, if I want to post on Facebook and LinkedIn, but the time or date is different, then I need to create one for each platform separately, they cannot be duplicated to change the platform), this leads to unnecessary actions that eat up the time.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Deceptively simple, yet powerful and affordable social media planning service
description: We are quite pleased with Ocoya. The simplicity of the tools helps our process so we can focus on the strategic part and confidently delegate the deployment .
author: Ricardo O.
pros: We love the simplicity of the interface. After adding the social profiles that come available for your plan, it takes just a few clicks to produce content. The integrated plugin offers lots of templates you can use to start quickly. If your marketing team or your client allows, or if you feel uninspired you can also use the AI integration to generate relevant content. Although we have not used them yet, the automation and e-commerce integrations are a great addition for those organizations that need to integrate external tools.
cons: Not a con per se, but you need to think forward on how many social media profiles and the complexity of your team to get the right plan.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

review_title: Clean and Efficient UI with a Strong Future Potential
description: Overall, I really love the aesthetics and the structure of the app which is super pleasing to the eyes and easy to move around and quickly get the job done. I am able to create content rather quickly without having to leave the app and create content elsewhere and then having to come back to the app to schedule social media posts. This makes it so efficient and a real pleasure to work with Ocoya.
author: Anil A.
pros: Ocoya UI/UX is extremely clean and easy to use. Very efficient for creating content from within the app and then schedule quickly for automated social media posting on multiple channels.
cons: The software is in its initial stages of development, but they are moving rather fast. They have some "growing pains" in the form some relatively small bugs, but I am expecting and hoping those get resolved fairly soon.

cons: With tactful resources, in a way the cheapest plans are well-capable and the best resources can come out very salty even more for those who have only one company or a single customer.
